title: Valenthyne Farfalla
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Update|[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]}} {{Conflicting|''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]''|''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]''}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:FarfallaHS.png]] |name=Valenthyne Farfalla |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[990 BBY]] ('''955BrS'''), [[Tython/Legends|Tython]]''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]'' |species=[[Half-Bothan]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]'' |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Blond]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents=[[Valenthyne Farfalla's father]][[Jedi vs. Sith 4|''Jedi vs. Sith'' 4]] |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' **[[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]][[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *[[House Farfalla]] |masters= |apprentices=[[Johun Othone]] }} {{Quote|I swore a vow to General Hoth. I promised I would not rest until the Sith had been cleansed from the galaxy. I still intend to honor that vow.|Valenthyne Farfalla|}} '''Valenthyne Farfalla''' was a noted [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[half-Bothan]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the [[Golden Age of the Old Republic]]. Farfalla had a reputation for being foppish and vain and fought for the [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]] as a [[Jedi Lord]] during the [[New Sith Wars]]. During the final years of the [[Ruusan campaign]], Farfalla left [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] to gather [[Farfalla's Knights|three hundred followers]] to aid in the struggle for the world. Farfalla was often at odds with Lord [[Hoth (Jedi Lord)|Hoth]]. When the latter's good friend [[Pernicar]] was killed by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], he blamed Farfalla, and the two had a falling out. However, they eventually repaired their relationship and rallied the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] for a final assault on the Sith stronghold. When Hoth and many other Jedi were killed by [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Lord]] [[Skere Kaan]]'s [[thought bomb]], Lords Farfalla and [[Berethon]] were the highest-ranking Jedi to survive Ruusan. Farfalla continued to serve the Jedi Order for a decade during the Golden Age of the Old Republic, until he was killed by [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] in a [[Duel on Tython|lightsaber battle]] on [[Tython/Legends|Tython]]. ==Biography== ===Early years=== {{Quote|Jedi is as Jedi does, Lord! And if he has taste…|Farfalla|}} [[File:Farfalla-JvS6.jpg|right|230px|Valenthyne Farfalla holding his lightsaber]] The son of a noble [[Jedi Lord]], Valenthyne Farfalla's [[Valenthyne Farfalla's father|father]] was convinced to ally with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] Pernicar. The young [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Hybrid/Legends|hybrid]] followed in his father's footsteps, [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] within the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and inheriting his father's lordship over a region of space he was sworn to defend from the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]. In [[1010 BBY]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and Lord [[Hoth (Jedi Lord)|Hoth]] departed [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and rallied the banners of the [[Jedi Grand Council]], summoning all Jedi Lords and their followers to form the [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]] to combat the threat of [[Skere Kaan]] and his [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]]. Commanding a [[Farfalla's Knights|legion]] of a hundred [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] and two hundred more loyal servants, Farfalla joined Hoth's Army aboard his ornate, [[Wroshyr wood/Legends|wroshyr-wood]]-hulled battleship the ''[[Fairwind]]''. During the decade of fighting, Lord Farfalla fought alongside the Jedi [[Kopecz]] before his defection to the Brotherhood. Because of his well-manicured appearance, Lord Farfalla could often be underestimated as a military threat, particularly by Lord Hoth, with whom, due to their widely differing personalities, he was often at odds. But despite his reputation amongst some, it was Farfalla who warned [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] [[Kiel Charny]] of [[Githany]]'s impending defection to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], saving the Jedi Knight from death. Farfalla was considered for the role of [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]]; however, he refused it in order to serve in the [[Ruusan campaign]]. As a result, [[Tarsus Valorum/Legends|Tarsus Valorum]] was appointed to that role, the first non-Jedi in 400 years to serve as such. ===Ruusan campaign=== [[File:Valenthyne_Farfalla.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Valenthyne Farfalla above ''[[Fairwind]]'', his flagship]] When Hoth began his attack on [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], Farfalla was one of the first Lords to rally to his side and would go on to fight in nearly every battle on the world, and his skill did not go unnoticed among the Sith. As the campaign ground to a stalemate, Hoth realized he needed the aid of the Jedi who had not chosen to join him. Farfalla believed that Hoth's blunt personality had driven them away, and expected that he could persuade them with more diplomacy and tact. In [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]] he volunteered to leave Ruusan to request help from Jedi throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and return with three hundred Knights. Farfalla would be gone for several months, in which time he led the three hundred Jedi against [[King/Legends|King]] [[Lahzar]], then took his forces on to Ruusan. The Sith had established a blockade around the planet, which prevented Farfalla from landing on the planet's surface. In a stroke of fortune for the Jedi, [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] went behind the back of Lord Kaan and ordered the Sith fleet to engage Farfalla's ships, giving Farfalla his opening to break through to Ruusan. There, he once again saved Charny's life, riding his golden warship into battle to defeat the attacking Sith.''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]'' and ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' differ on the circumstances of Farfalla's arrival. ''Path of Destruction'' has him arriving for the first time before [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] arrives on Ruusan, and only bringing his fleet to the surface later, while ''Jedi vs. Sith'' shows him and his fleet arriving at the same time after Darth Bane has arrived and laid waste to the surface of Ruusan with the other [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords]]. Furthermore, in the former he has reconciled with Hoth by the time his Knights arrive, while in the latter they are still antagonistic. This article incorporates the elements either common to both stories or uncontradicted by either; specific decisions as to which account is canon where direct contradictions exist have not been announced. ===Rift with Hoth=== His arrival was not welcomed by Lord Hoth, however, who called Farfalla a "mincing coward." That Farfalla had arrived just too late to save Hoth's good friend Pernicar only added to the resentment between the two men, and in a fit of anger, Farfalla returned to his ship, vowing not to help Hoth for what he saw as a grave insult, and also fearing that Hoth's personal conflict with Kaan and the death of Pernicar had pushed the general to the dark side of the Force. However, when an image of Pernicar came to Hoth in a dream suggesting he mend his relationship with Farfalla, the Jedi sent a message via [[Unidentified envoy|Farfalla's envoy]] apologizing for his behavior. The two came to a truce and joined forces to launch a final assault against Kaan, who had fled to the safety of the hills and caves. Though both Jedi suspected a trap, they elected to pursue. ===Reconciliation and the thought bomb=== {{Quote|Promise me death, Farfalla, and receive a warning in return.''"
"''You wish death, Dark Lord? Just say so!''"
"''Death is an old friend. What Kaan plans for all of us is ''worse''!|Kopecz challenges Farfalla|}} [[File:Battleofruusan.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Farfalla triumphant in the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan]]]] Farfalla led the advance group of Jedi at the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan]] to engage the Sith forces, while Hoth traveled closely behind, intending to continue on to the caves to apprehend Kaan. The battle was over quickly, with most Sith surrendering to the Army of Light. During the battle, Farfalla captured the Dark Lord Kopecz, who told him that Kaan was planning to detonate a thought bomb, a devastating burst of [[The Force/Legends|Force]] energy that would eradicate any army caught within its blast range. Farfalla fulfilled Kopecz's wish to die by granting him a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] duel, then rushed to inform Hoth. Hoth already knew, as he had felt it in the Force, but decided he had to stop Kaan and took ninety-nine volunteers with him. He ordered Farfalla and his forces to flee the area, and if possible, leave Ruusan, hoping to give them a chance to escape certain death. While a traumatized soldier later told [[Darovit]] that all the Jedi had been killed, Farfalla was well out of range when Kaan detonated the thought bomb, and so survived the Ruusan campaign, rallying the surviving Jedi to defeat the Sith stragglers and finally ending the thousand-year New Sith Wars. Following the death of Lord Hoth, Farfalla became the last commander of the Republic military before the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]]. He also regularly conferred with the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]]. ===Aftermath of Ruusan=== {{Quote|On Coruscant, appearance matters.|Valenthyne Farfalla|}} [[File:Duel_on_Tython.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Farfalla battles Darth Zannah during the duel on Tython.]] Farfalla commandeered the rescue missions from the ''[[Fairwind]]'', sending teams like those aboard ''[[Star-Wake]]'' to Ruusan. Later, Farfalla met with [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Tarsus Valorum/Legends|Tarsus Valorum]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where the two agreed to the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformations]]. Following the meeting, Farfalla put [[Johun Othone]] in charge of Valorum's security detail, and knighted the young Padawan, whom he'd taken as his own following Hoth's death. For a decade, Farfalla continued to head various operations of the Jedi Order. Although he had dismissed rumors of a surviving Sith Lord from Ruusan over a decade prior, Farfalla eventually was convinced a Sith Lord still existed when Darovit came forward with information. However, before Darovit could speak with them, the disguised [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Darth Zannah]] took her cousin with her to [[Tython/Legends|Tython]], to avoid being revealed and caught by the Jedi. Farfalla, Othone, [[Raskta Lsu]], [[Worror Dowmat]], and [[Sarro Xaj]] pursued the [[Loranda (starship)|''Loranda'']] aboard the ''[[Justice Crusader]]'', where they came to Tython and engaged in a [[Duel on Tython|duel]] with [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] and Darth Zannah. At first, thanks to Worror's [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]], the duel was in the favor of the Jedi. However, after Worror lost his concentration, the Sith Lords easily took control of the fight. Farfalla was killed after Bane disarmed and subsequently [[Sai cha/Legends|decapitated]] him. Farfalla's lightsaber was taken as a prize for the victory. ===Legacy=== Following Farfalla's death on Tython, the Jedi High Council, alerted to the existence of Darth Bane by [[Caleb]] of [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]], sent a [[Ambria strike team|team]] of Jedi—led by Master [[Tho'natu]], and including Master [[Obba]]—to destroy him. Outside of [[Caleb's hut|Caleb's home]] on Ambria, the Jedi discovered the healer's cut up corpse, and inside they found the [[Lightsaber hilt/Legends|lightsaber hilts]] of the slain Jedi. Tho'natu announced their presence and they were instantly assaulted by a deranged Darovit wielding Farfalla's lightsaber, whom they assumed to be the injured Bane. [[Confrontation on Ambria|Slaying him]], they deemed that the Sith were extinct and returned the lightsaber hilts and the decimated corpse of Caleb to the Temple. After returning to the Temple, the Jedi created a memorial to Caleb and the Jedi who had been killed by the Sith Lord. All five Jedi's lightsaber hilts were set into a large white stone block in the gardens around the base of the [[Tower of First Knowledge/Legends|Tower of First Knowledge]]. Under each hilt was a depiction of its wielder, along with their name.''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]'' An [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]] in the [[Ruusan system/Legends|Ruusan system]] was named [[Farfalla's Diamonds]] in his honor.{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale5|text=Light and Dark: Kyle Katarn's Tale, Mission 5|int=Kyle Katarn's Tale}} ==Personality and traits== [[File:HothFarfalla.jpg|thumb|right|210px|Farfalla was often at odds with Lord Hoth.]] {{Dialogue|Hoth|Why should they listen to you any more than me?|Farfalla|My Lord, for all your skill in battle and all your strength in the Force, you are somewhat lacking in the delicate art of diplomacy. You are a brilliant general, and your taciturn nature serves you well when giving orders to your troops. Unfortunately, it can set those who are not under your command on edge.|Pernicar|You are too blunt, my Lord.|Farfalla|That's what I just said…|attr=Farfalla, Hoth and Pernicar|src=}} Valenthyne Farfalla was deeply concerned with appearance and behavior. He dressed in fine clothes, gold, and precious gems, spoke in a daring hero tone, and adopted aristocratic and flamboyant mannerisms. Some, including Lord Hoth, saw him as useless or playing hero because of this, while others found Farfalla witty or charming. Farfalla was gifted in the art of natural persuasion even over other Jedi. Farfalla himself believed he was simply showing a good sense of taste. Valenthyne had a strong sense of honor even assembling a task force of Jedi to eliminate Darth Bane without telling the council as to finish his promise to Hoth. ==Powers and abilities== Valenthyne Farfalla was a Jedi Master of many talents. He acted as a general during the Sith war, leading three hundred soldiers and then assuming command of the entire Army of Light upon Hoth's death. While fighting on Ruusan, he wielded a blue-bladed lightsaber. In the years after the war, Farfalla wielded a gold-bladed saber. In combat, he adopted a graceful and defensive stance against his enemies. His fighting style was described as elegant and clean, characteristics similar with those of the [[Form II/Legends|Makashi]] style of dueling.[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]] Farfalla's skill with a lightsaber was less than that of his Jedi Weapons Master Raskta Lsu, a fact made obvious when she regarded him as a hindrance when they dueled against Darth Bane. His skills also were not enough to defeat those of Bane, who twice disarmed the Jedi Master in combat. Farfalla chose to use the Force for defense of himself and his allies. His skill allowed him to create Force barriers, which he used to assist his fellow Masters on Tython. When invigorated by Worror's battle meditation, Farfalla felt quicker and more powerful. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Jedi_Temple_stained_glass_concept_art.png|thumb|right|250px|Jedi Temple stained glass concept art in ''Star Wars: The Clone Wars The Complete Season Two'']] In ''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]'', Farfalla is depicted in a panel dying from the effects of the "thought bomb." ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' states, "both [the Brotherhood of Darkness and the Army of Light] were wiped out by Lord Kaan's thought bomb," indicating Farfalla also perished at this time. However, in [[Drew Karpyshyn]]'s novel ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'', Farfalla, as well as hundreds of Jedi in the Army of Light, survives the "thought bomb's" effects by fleeing the surface of Ruusan. When asked about Farfalla's species in the [[StarWars.com Message Boards]], [[Leland Chee]] did not answer concretely but suggested that "all pointy-eared species are either Sephi or closely related to Sephi."{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1395#16675997|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2007-06-08|time=6:48 AM|quote=Not concretely. For the longest time we've used dozens of pointy-eared characters without a species name for them. The pointed-eared species in the Jedi: Yoda comic were named Sephi. My theory is that all pointy-eared species are either Sephi or closely related to Sephi.'}} Valenthyne Farfalla appeared in the artwork by [[Wayne Lo]] titled "Unused Jedi Temple mosaic" for the [[The Clone Wars: Season Two|second season]] of the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series. It was included in the production journal of the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars The Complete Season Two]]''. The concept art also depicted Lord Hoth and the death of Pernicar.[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars The Complete Season Two|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' The Complete Season Two]] Parts of the mosaic can be seen, which includes Farfalla's leg, in a [[Unidentified veranda (Jedi Temple)|room]] in "[[Lightsaber Lost]]," the eleventh episode of the second season of the series.{{TCW|Lightsaber Lost}}
